items.briefing:
Australian shoppers are being urged to stay cautious when buying through social media platforms, with research showing local consumers have concerns about security on social commerce sites. Separately, Australia's Signals Directorate (ASD) has issued a warning about "device code phishing" — a scam technique where criminals trick you into handing over account access by impersonating login processes. Criminal toolkits enabling this attack are reportedly growing.

Apple also blocked over $2.2 billion in fraudulent App Store transactions globally last year, a reminder to only download apps from trusted sources. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected login code or authentication request you didn't initiate, do not approve it — contact your IT team immediately.

featured.title: ASD warns of device code phishing as Proofpoint tracks growing criminal toolkits
featured.quote: ASD warning on device code phishing targets Australian Microsoft 365 users with active threat.
